meta.setting: old campfire ring beside a wagon track and pine duff in Jefferson country
meta.rdr_clone_target: PS3-era RDR-clone western art, grounded in trail evidence and night-camp pressure

research_grounding.title: NPS Whiskeytown History and Culture
research_grounding.use: Rugged road travel and outdoor working conditions.

research_grounding.title: California Department of Fish and Wildlife Black Bear

image_prompt.subject: an old campfire ring with gray ash, coffee grounds, dented tin cup, bedroll marks, boot scuffs, horse tracks, and bear sign at the pine edge
image_prompt.scene: low evening light after travelers have left; smoke stain clings to stones, playing cards are half-buried in duff, no readable markings
image_prompt.composition: square evidence scene with fire ring in foreground, bedroll dents mid-frame, wagon track and dark pines beyond
image_prompt.camera: low tracker-view angle, close enough to read ash, prints, and abandoned camp objects
image_prompt.hand_cut_note: Make the empty camp feel recently occupied and useful to read.

style_modifiers.medium: PS3-era open-world western game art, early-HD in-engine trail-evidence scene
render_traits: slightly simplified camp objects, tracks, pine edge, and ground texture
modest texture resolution on ash, stone, tin, wool, leather marks, mud, and pine duff
hard ambient occlusion under stones, cup, card corners, bedroll dents, and brush
low warm light with cool shadows under the timber
believable 1899 camp gear and trail wear
evidence-focused composition that supports tracking and social inference
style_modifiers.palette: ash gray, coal black, tin dull silver, pine green, mud umber, blanket brown, dust ochre, and low evening gold.

text_rendering.instructions: No readable labels, card faces, paper scraps, brands, or text.

negative_prompt: copy of commercial game characters, missions, towns, or logos

self_check: Reads as PS3-era western game art at square page-card scale.
The fire ring, ash, camp objects, bedroll marks, tracks, and pine edge are readable.
Every visible object belongs plausibly to rugged 1899 camp life.
The image supports tracking, rest, rumor, night danger, and missing-traveler scenes.
No copied game characters, readable modern text, or inherited non-western symbols.
